message:
bd-12f4: E2E TUI smoke tests with fixture data and artifact capture

Add 6 comprehensive E2E smoke tests to the TUI module:

- e2e_smoke_full_interaction_flow: 27-step scripted session traversing
  all views (Home→Panes→Events→Triage→Search→Help) with navigation,
  filtering, workflow expand/collapse, search query/execute/clear, and
  tab cycling. Captures full session transcript as artifact.

- e2e_smoke_resize_stability: renders all 6 views at 6 different
  terminal sizes (40x10 to 200x60) verifying no panics.

- e2e_smoke_rapid_key_sequence: fires 29 rapid key presses exercising
  pane navigation, filters, triage actions, search, tab cycling, and
  refresh — rendering after every key.

- e2e_smoke_pane_view_renders_all_fixture_data: verifies all 3 fixture
  panes appear in rendered output.

- e2e_smoke_workflow_panel_in_triage: verifies workflow progress panel
  renders with running workflow data.

- e2e_smoke_home_view_shows_health: verifies health/status info appears.

Uses FixtureQueryClient with 3 panes, 3 events, 2 triage items, and
1 active workflow. Buffer-based rendering with text extraction for
assertions. 114 TUI tests total now pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-13y1","title":"Config validation + wa notify test","description":"## What
Extend notifications config and add a CLI test command.

## Why
Misconfigured channels should fail fast with actionable errors and users need a safe connectivity check.

## How
- Extend existing [notifications] config with channel definitions
- CLI: `wa notify test --channel <name>` returns structured success/failure
- Reuse NotificationGate for filters and cooldown

## Success Criteria
- Invalid configs produce clear error messages
- Test command validates redaction and endpoint reachability","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:19.633409931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T09:18:11.609739890Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T09:18:11.609661384Z","close_reason":"Added notifications config validation and new 'wa notify test --channel' with gate/redaction/delivery checks; added tests; ran fmt/check/clippy/test.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13y1","depends_on_id":"bd-2uyz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-13y1","depends_on_id":"bd-ugaj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-13y1","author":"SilentCanyon","text":"Implemented notifications config validation (severity/agent types, webhook name/URL/duplicates) and added  command with gate+redaction+delivery checks plus CLI parse test. Ran cargo fmt/check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings, cargo test.","created_at":"2026-02-04T09:17:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-14da","title":"CLI: wa secrets scan/report","description":"## What
Add CLI commands to run scans and view reports.

## Why
Operators need a simple workflow to verify secret hygiene.

## How
- `wa secrets scan` runs scan and stores report
- `wa secrets report` prints counts and redacted locations

## Success Criteria
- Output is fully redacted and includes counts by pattern","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:30.346782578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:27:56.247328688Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14da","depends_on_id":"bd-2pii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-14da","depends_on_id":"bd-2yoy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-14da","depends_on_id":"bd-5wge","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:21Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-15ds","title":"[EPIC] Local RPC socket for robot/MCP (auth + caps)","description":"## Background
Robot/MCP APIs are powerful; local tools need a secure and ergonomic way to connect without parsing CLI output.

## Goals
- Provide a local IPC server over Unix domain socket / named pipe
- Authenticate with short-lived tokens and capability scopes
- Keep parity with robot/MCP schemas

## Non-Goals
- Remote network exposure (handled by distributed mode)

## Considerations
- Must be disabled by default
- Requires strict redaction and audit logging

## Success Criteria
- Local client can call core robot APIs over IPC
- Token and scope checks enforced
- Unit + e2e tests for auth and schema parity","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:13.180280826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:13.180280826Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.6","title":"[Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export)","description":"# Task: `wa diag bundle`

## Goal
Provide the CLI surface described in the plan:
- `wa diag bundle --last 15m --output <path>`

This should wrap the diagnostic bundle generator (`wa-nu4.3.4.3`) and ensure the UX is great:
- clear progress logging
- clear output location
- stable exit codes

## Requirements
- Redaction always on by default.
- If requested output path exists, refuse unless `--force`.
- Include enabled features + versions in the bundle.

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.3.4.7` (bundle tests) and should include:
  - CLI exit codes and refusal behavior (`--force`)
  - stable metadata fields
  - no secrets in stdout/stderr or bundle contents

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `wa diag bundle` produces a bundle containing:
  - config summary (redacted)
  - versions
  - recent events + workflow logs
  - health snapshot
  - audit excerpts (if enabled)

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.7","title":"Diagnostic bundle tests: redaction, file layout, stable metadata (no secrets)","description":"# Task: Diagnostic bundle tests

## Goal
Ensure diagnostic bundles are reliable and safe.

## Testing
- Bundle layout contains required files.
- Redaction applied (no secrets in outputs).
- Stable metadata present (`version`, `enabled_features`).
- Reservation snapshot present when reservations exist (owner/TTL, redacted).
- If bundle already exists and `--force` not set, command fails safely.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are deterministic and do not require a running WezTerm instance (use fixture DB and fixture config).


LABELS: area-diagnostics, area-tests, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4: [EPIC] Diagnostics: wa doctor/status health, debug bundle, performance budgets ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4.3: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.4.6: [Human command] `wa diag bundle` (wrapper around sanitized diagnostic bundle export) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","title":"Doctor tests: fixture-based checks + output stability (JSON/TTY)","description":"# Task: `wa doctor` tests

## Goal
Make `wa doctor` a **trustworthy** and **regression-resistant** diagnostic surface.

`wa doctor` is how users decide whether to trust the tool. If it flakes, lies, or leaks secrets, we lose users.

## Test scope

### Unit tests
- Path resolution rendering (workspace root, DB/log/lock paths) is correct and deterministic.
- JSON output shape is stable (fields present, naming stable, no secret leakage).
- Redaction is applied to any potentially sensitive field (even on error paths).

### Integration tests (fixture-driven; no real WezTerm required)
Run `wa doctor` in a hermetic test environment that simulates:
- A healthy system.
- Missing `wezterm` binary / `wezterm cli list` failure.
- Workspace not writable.
- DB unreadable / schema mismatch.
- Watcher not running / lock held / stale lock.
- Optional feature checks present/absent (mcp/browser/web/tui) without forcing those deps.

## Test strategy / harness design
- Prefer dependency injection where feasible (traits) so we can simulate:
  - `wezterm` CLI calls
  - storage open/migration version
  - watcher health snapshot
- If process-based stubbing is simpler, use a test harness that:
  - writes a small “fake wezterm” executable to a temp dir
  - prepends that temp dir to `PATH`
  - feeds deterministic stdout/stderr for specific subcommands

## Output stability requirements
- Non-TTY mode:
  - `wa doctor --format json` must be strictly machine-parseable and stable.
  - Tests validate JSON against a versioned schema (if/when we add a schema for doctor output).
- TTY mode:
  - Tests validate key content (not exact ANSI art); either strip ANSI or snapshot a normalized rendering.

## Logging & debuggability
- Tests must capture logs on failure and print them (bounded) to make failures diagnosable.
- Ensure logs do not leak secrets (redaction applied to any dynamic content).

## Deliverables
- Unit test module(s) for doctor output + redaction.
- Integration test harness utilities for stubbing wezterm/storage/watcher.
- A small set of golden fixtures covering the major failure modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa doctor` has coverage for each major check and for each major failure mode.
- Tests do not require:
  - network access
  - a real WezTerm instance
  - a real user home directory
- Adding a new doctor check requires adding at least one test covering both success and failure paths.

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure the integration harness actually uses the fake `wezterm` binary (assert invocation).
  - Add an explicit assertion that `--format json` output contains no ANSI escapes and validates against schema (once added).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayRidge","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:17:30.311960939Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:18:16.728019600Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:18:16.727950151Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-diagnostics","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.8","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.5","title":"[EPIC] Export & reporting: wa export (JSONL/NDJSON), DB snapshots, summaries","description":"# [EPIC] Export & reporting

## Mission
Make captured data portable and useful outside wa:
- export history slices
- share reproducible evidence for bugs
- generate summaries/reports

## Guiding principles
- Exports must be stable formats (JSONL/NDJSON) with version fields.
- Apply redaction by default.
- Prefer incremental exports over monolithic dumps.

## Definition of done
- `wa export` can export:
  - segments (with seq + timestamps)
  - gaps
  - events/detections
  - workflow executions + step logs
- Optional: export DB snapshot safely.


## Success Criteria
- Export paths (JSONL/NDJSON, DB snapshot, reports) are safe, redacted, and reproducible.
- Export artifacts include enough context to debug workflows/policy decisions without leaking secrets.
- Tests validate redaction and snapshot correctness; E2E validates end-to-end export flows.


## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - Export format versioning and schema validation for each JSONL stream.
  - Redaction-by-default (and explicit opt-out behavior if we allow it).

- Integration tests:
  - Export from a fixture DB and assert stable ordering and completeness.

- E2E:
  - At least one E2E script must prove exports are included in artifact bundles and are secret-safe (`wa-4vx.10.18`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.1","title":"[Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters)","description":"# Task: Human command `wa export`

## Goal
Export wa’s captured data into stable, composable formats.

## Export formats
- JSONL/NDJSON (one JSON object per line)
- Optional pretty JSON for small outputs

## Data to export
- output segments (including seq)
- output gaps
- events/detections (including rule_id/pack)
- workflow executions + step logs
- **pane reservations (active + historical)**

## Filters
- `--pane-id`
- `--since` / `--until`
- `--limit`

## Safety
- Apply redaction by default.
- Provide an explicit `--no-redact` that is hard to misuse (confirm / warn).

## Testing
- Export tests (`wa-nu4.3.5.4`) must cover:
  - stable schemas/versioning
  - filtering correctness
  - redaction/no-secrets guarantees
  - reservation export schema + redaction

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exported JSONL can be re-ingested into analysis tools without additional context.


LABELS: area-cli, area-export, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.2: Implement StorageHandle (single writer thread + read pool) with bounded write queue ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.5: Persist events, agent_sessions, workflow_executions, workflow_step_log ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5: [EPIC] Export & reporting: wa export (JSONL/NDJSON), DB snapshots, summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.3: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.5.4: Export tests + schema docs (versioned JSONL formats) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.5.5: Export audit trail (JSONL): actions, policy decisions, verification summaries ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.5.3: Generate reports: session summary, compaction timeline, workflow traces (Markdown) ● P3
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:27:53.083599221Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T16:16:32.701317110Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T16:16:12.540839238Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-export","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented wa export CLI command with JSONL/NDJSON output. Created export module (export.rs) with ExportKind enum, JSONL streaming, redaction. Added 5 storage query methods with dynamic filter builder. Added Export CLI variant with --pane-id, --since, --until, --limit, --no-redact, --pretty, --output flags. 10 passing tests. Also fixed pre-existing clap issue on Auth variant.","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:16:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.2","title":"DB snapshot export (safe copy, WAL handling, size checks)","description":"# Task: DB snapshot export

## Goal
Allow exporting the wa SQLite database as a portable artifact for backup/debugging.

This supports:
- user self-service bug reports
- deterministic repro bundles
- safe backups for long-running watcher instances

## Safety requirements
- Must not corrupt or block the live DB.
- Must handle WAL mode correctly.
  - Prefer SQLite backup API (consistent snapshot) or a safe `VACUUM INTO` style approach.
  - If checkpointing is used, do it intentionally and document the tradeoffs.
- Must validate output size and warn if unexpectedly large.
- Must never include secrets beyond what already exists in the DB (and DB should already be redacted where required).

## Deliverables
- A human-facing entrypoint (name flexible):
  - `wa export db-snapshot --out <path>`
  - (or `wa export --db <path>`)
- Optional compression (feature-gated) for large snapshots.
- A short metadata sidecar (optional) that includes:
  - wa version
  - schema version
  - created_at
  - workspace id/path (redacted)

## Logging / UX
- Progress logs (bounded, non-spammy):
  - starting snapshot
  - checkpoint/backup method chosen
  - bytes written
  - completion path
- On failure: actionable error + suggestion to run `wa diag bundle`.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - output path validation (no directory traversal)
  - metadata rendering (if implemented)

- Integration tests:
  - create a temp DB with known rows
  - run snapshot export
  - open exported DB read-only and verify expected tables/rows exist
  - verify WAL handling does not leave a corrupt or partial snapshot

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exported DB can be opened read-only and contains expected tables.
- Export is safe while watcher is running (no corruption; bounded blocking).
- Failures are actionable and do not leak secrets in logs.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.3","title":"Generate reports: session summary, compaction timeline, workflow traces (Markdown)","description":"# Task: Generate human-readable reports

## Goal
Turn raw history into actionable summaries for humans:
- what happened
- when
- what wa did
- what remains unhandled

## Reports (initial)
- Session report per pane (time-bounded):
  - major events
  - workflow runs + outcomes
  - gaps
- Compaction report:
  - compaction detected
  - context injection sent
  - verification result

## Output
- Markdown by default.
- JSON metadata sidecar optional.

## Testing
- Fixture-driven tests:
  - generate reports from a small fixture DB
  - snapshot/compare markdown output (stable headings + ordering)
  - assert redaction: no raw secrets or transcript content beyond allowed snippets

## Acceptance Criteria
- A report generated from a fixture DB is readable and self-contained.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.4","title":"Export tests + schema docs (versioned JSONL formats)","description":"# Task: Export tests + schema documentation

## Goal
Make exported formats trustworthy and long-lived.

## Deliverables
- Unit tests for:
  - correct filtering
  - correct redaction behavior
  - stable field naming
  - **reservation export schema + redaction**
- Documentation:
  - what each exported record type contains
  - versioning strategy

## Acceptance Criteria
- `bd lint` / project lint passes and export tests cover at least one fixture end-to-end.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Validate exported JSONL against JSON Schemas in tests (not just snapshots).
  - Add a “schema drift” test that fails if an export field is removed/renamed without bumping the version.


LABELS: area-export, area-tests, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5: [EPIC] Export & reporting: wa export (JSONL/NDJSON), DB snapshots, summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.1: [Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.2: DB snapshot export (safe copy, WAL handling, size checks) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.5","title":"Export audit trail (JSONL): actions, policy decisions, verification summaries","description":"# Task: Export audit trail

## Goal
Include audit actions in exports so bugs are reproducible and postmortems are complete.

## Output
- JSONL/NDJSON with stable schema and version field.

## Filters
- `--pane-id`
- `--since` / `--until`
- `--actor`

## Safety
- Redaction applied.

## Coverage expectations
- Export includes reservation-related audit rows (conflicts, approvals) alongside send/workflow actions.

## Testing
- Export tests (`wa-nu4.3.5.4`) must cover:
  - stable field naming + versioning
  - correct filtering
  - redaction/no-secrets guarantees

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa export` can include audit records for a time window and the output is parseable.


LABELS: area-audit, area-export, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.8: Audit trail storage: audit_actions table + queries + retention/redaction hooks ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5: [EPIC] Export & reporting: wa export (JSONL/NDJSON), DB snapshots, summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.1: [Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters) ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-4vx.10.16: E2E script: RequireApproval → wa approve allow-once → send succeeds (audited) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-4vx.10.18: E2E script: secret redaction in audit/export (no raw secrets in artifacts) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-4vx.10.25: E2E script: command safety gate blocks destructive-looking sends (optional dcg) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.3: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events ● P2
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T10:00:29.028808369Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T16:25:16.372931267Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T16:24:52.336754217Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-audit","area-export","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.5","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.5","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":176,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.5.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Audit trail export fully implemented via wa export audit command. Added --actor and --action CLI filters for audit-specific filtering (mapped to AuditQuery.actor_kind and AuditQuery.action_kind). Redaction applied via AuditActionRecord.redact_fields(). 2 new tests: actor/action filtering, and redaction of decision_reason/input_summary fields. Unblocks wa-4vx.10.16, wa-4vx.10.18, wa-4vx.10.25, wa-nu4.3.4.3.","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:25:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.6","title":"[EPIC] Optional web server (fastapi_rust): read-only dashboard + health","description":"# [EPIC] Optional web server (fastapi_rust)

## Mission
Expose a small HTTP interface for dashboards and lightweight integrations.

## Principles
- Start read-only.
- Share the same underlying data queries as CLI/robot/MCP.
- Feature-gated (`--features web`).

## Scope (initial)
- `/health`
- `/panes`
- `/events`
- `/search`

## Non-goals
- Remote control (send text) over HTTP in v0.1.

## Definition of done
- `wa web` starts server and serves the read-only endpoints.


## Success Criteria
- Web dashboard is read-only by default and uses the same query layer as robot/human surfaces.
- Health endpoints and basic views (panes/events/search) are fast and safe.
- Tests cover auth/offline modes and ensure no mutation endpoints exist by default.


## Testing
- Endpoint contract tests:
  - Validate JSON shapes for /health, /panes, /events, /search.

- Safety tests:
  - Assert the server binds to localhost by default and refuses to bind to 0.0.0.0 unless explicitly requested.
  - Assert there are no endpoints that mutate panes (send text) when web feature is enabled.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:29:06.148369148Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T15:44:04.119445333Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-integrations","area-web","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
